---
title: "Teaching and Mentoring"
---

<!-- Google Fonts -->
<link href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Inter:wght@300;400;500&family=Playfair+Display:wght@500;600&display=swap" rel="stylesheet">

<style>
body {
  font-family: 'Inter', sans-serif;
  color: #333;
}

.hero a {
  color: #b45373;
  text-decoration: none;
  font-weight: 500;
}

.hero a:hover {
  text-decoration: underline;
}

.content a {
  color: #b45373;
  text-decoration: none;
}

.content a:hover {
  text-decoration: underline;
}

h1 {
  font-family: 'Playfair Display', serif;
  font-weight: 600;
  letter-spacing: 0.3px;
}

h3 {
  font-weight: 500;
}

.content {
  line-height: 1.7;
}

.news-cards {
  max-width: 760px;
  margin: 0 auto 64px;
  display: grid;
  gap: 14px;
}

.news-card {
  display: block;
  padding: 16px 18px;
  border-radius: 12px;
  border: 1px solid #f2cbd4;
  background: #ffffff;
  text-decoration: none;
  color: inherit;
  transition: box-shadow 0.15s ease, transform 0.15s ease;
}

.news-card:hover {
  transform: translateY(-1px);
  box-shadow: 0 6px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.05);
}

.news-card strong {
  display: block;
  font-size: 1.05em;
  line-height: 1.4;
}

.news-card span {
  display: block;
  margin-top: 6px;
  font-size: 0.9em;
  color: #666;
}
</style>

<!-- HERO -->
<div class="hero" style="
  text-align: center;
  padding: 64px 20px 48px;
  background: linear-gradient(180deg, #fdecef 0%, #ffffff 100%);
  border-bottom: 1px solid #f2cbd4;
  margin-bottom: 48px;
">

*Training statisticians and collaborating scientists through coursework, hands‑on research, and mentorship.*

I teach graduate‑level courses in biostatistics and mentor masters and PhD students on methodological and applied research projects. I emphasize conceptual understanding of complex analyses and collaborative problem solving in scientific settings.

</div>

<!-- MAIN CONTENT -->
<div class="content" style="
  max-width: 780px;
  margin: 0 auto 48px;
  font-size: 1.1em;
">

## Students and Research Team

In addition to teaching, I mentor several students on masters‑ and PhD‑level research projects.  
If you are a current University of Iowa student interested in my work, please contact me.  
Prospective students are encouraged to apply through the **[Department of Biostatistics graduate program](https://www.public-health.uiowa.edu/biostatistics-faq/)**.

#### PhD Students

Stephanie Jansson

#### Masters Preceptorships

**[Amy Wu](https://www.public-health.uiowa.edu/seminar/preceptorship-reports-amy-wu-jake-alston-and-keeley-powers/)**  
Survival and mediation analysis of race, telomere length, and cancer mortality  
Fall 2023

**[Dosten Kpozehouen](https://www.public-health.uiowa.edu/seminar/preceptorship-reports-mahoney-kpozehouen-jiang-and-johnson/)**

Prediction of survival time from treatment with application to pancreatic cancer  
Spring 2024

**[Ryan Colburn](https://www.public-health.uiowa.edu/seminar/preceptorship-reports-colburn-hurley-and-shelton/)**  
Surrogate Validation for Prostate Cancer Survival
Fall 2025

**[Brandon Gerhardt](https://www.public-health.uiowa.edu/seminar/preceptorship-reports-gerhardt-and-kakou/)**  
Integrating external controls into type 1 diabetes randomized clinical trial data  

Fall 2025

**[Tian Zhao](https://www.public-health.uiowa.edu/seminar/preceptorship-presentations-zhao-steinmetz-wallace-and-tertyshnaia/)**

Bayesian Methods for Futility Analysis in Prostate Cancer Trials

Spring 2026

**[Braden Wallace](https://www.public-health.uiowa.edu/seminar/preceptorship-presentations-zhao-steinmetz-wallace-and-tertyshnaia/)**

Causal Inference for Non Compliance
Spring 2026

## Lab News and Student Recognition

<div class="news-cards">

  <a class="news-card"
     href="https://www.public-health.uiowa.edu/news-items/recipients-of-dr-carol-s-gleich-development-award-announced-5/"
     target="_blank">
    <strong>Amy Wu receives the Carol S. Gleich Development Award</strong>
    <span>Award supports presentation of her research at ENAR 2024 in Baltimore</span>
  </a>

  <a class="news-card"
     href="https://x.com/asa_iowa/status/1780578523196875194"
     target="_blank">
    <strong>Amy Wu wins Iowa Chapter of the ASA Best Poster Award</strong>
    <span>Recognized for outstanding student research, 2024</span>
  </a>

<div class="news-card">
  <strong>Dosten Kpozehouen presents at the University of Iowa Cancer Center retreat</strong>
  <span>Research presentation as part of the annual Cancer Center retreat, 2024</span>
</div>

<div class="news-card">
  <strong>Dosten Kpozehouen awarded Favorite Poster</strong>
  <span>2025 Annual Meeting of the Iowa Chapter of the American Statistical Association</span>
</div>

<div class="news-card">
  <strong>Stephanie Janssen presents poster</strong>
  <span>2026 Annual Meeting of the Iowa Chapter of the American Statistical Association</span>
</div>

</div>



## Graduate Courses

I teach graduate‑level biostatistics courses in the **[University of Iowa course catalog](https://catalog.registrar.uiowa.edu/courses/bios/)** to MS and PhD students in biostatistics and beyond.

#### BIOS:6650 Causal Inference  
Spring 2023, Spring 2024, Spring 2025, Spring 2026

Overview of causal inference with emphasis on observational research. Topics include counterfactual frameworks, causal graphs, time‑varying treatments and confounding, inverse probability weighting, doubly robust estimation, causal mediation analysis, and quantitative bias analysis.

#### BIOS:6310 Introductory Longitudinal Data Analysis  
Fall 2023, Fall 2024, Fall 2025

Introduction to statistical modeling of clustered and repeated‑measures data for continuous and non‑continuous outcomes. Emphasis on applications and computation using ANOVA‑based methods, hierarchical and mixed‑effects models, generalized estimating equations, and generalized linear mixed models.
